plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-560 RP18.P03.M04.S13-RP18.P03.M06.S10 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-560 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P03 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the eight remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first ten rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-561 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-560 is a descriptor-only AI Legal Workflows (RP18) closeout pack covering 40 planned units across RP18.P03.M04.S13-RP18.P03.M06.S10 (M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail 8 rows, M05 Permission And Audit Binding 22 rows, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head 10 rows). All eight in-scope implementation refs are mutually consistent: the authoritative closeout-pack-plan.json contains CP00-560 with matching unit_count/range/distribution; registry pack-binding, requirements, and no-write attestation align with the contract (validated via deepEqual in scripts/validate-rp18-ai-legal-workflows-core-contract.mjs); service descriptors are pure frozen-object construction; validators enforce coverage, descriptor-only row guards, and authority boundaries; and model.test.js asserts coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, and boundaries. The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly false (runtime_ready false, status implemented_pending_review); the Hermes production_ready_candidate flag is a distinct implementation-gate signal, not a final gate. No-write/no-real-data is verified both by the attestation flags and by the absence of any writeFile/fetch/exec/db operations in the package source. Claude is preserved as a read-only, non-final reviewer (claude_is_final_approval false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval false, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation), and Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false). Review-hardening controls are present: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth-failure/0-byte/malformed-JSON/fenced-JSON/tool-call-shaped/missing-field cases, auth preflight passed, and raw-output/receipt are correctly absent pre-review. All 27 validation commands in command-evidence.json passed (exit 0), including git diff --check. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, ldip invalid-attempt) are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout, which remain gated on the normal post-review adjudication/inspection steps and human final approval outside this receipt.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-560 ai legal workflows core p03 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
